Container.Name: Restore-Runner
Container.Overview:
Restore-test your Unraid AppData Backup archives in a click.
Upload a backup archive → it extracts → it spins up the backed-up container in an isolated sandbox → you preview its WebUI inline → one Stop button tears it down.
Supported formats: .zip, .rar, .tar, .tar.gz, .tar.zst
Features (v0.4): - Parses Unraid container XML to get image, ports, volumes, env - Ephemeral appdata: mounts are rewritten to point at the uploaded archive, never your live /mnt/user/appdata - Isolated internal docker network (rr-sandbox-net): no host ports, no LAN, no internet for the sandbox - WebUI reverse-proxied into the RestoreRunner UI (iframe + open-in-new-tab), WebSocket-capable - Auto-stops after a configurable timeout (default 10 min) - Live log streaming via Server-Sent Events - Relaunch button re-runs extracted archives without re-upload - Admin-only (bcrypt + session cookie, SameSite=Strict)
First-run: - Open the WebUI — the wizard will ask you to create an admin.
Pre-release beta — report issues on GitHub.
Container.DonateText: If you like my work, please consider a donation!

Config.Name: Config Dir
Config.Description: SQLite DB + extracted uploads + persisted logs.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required — RestoreRunner uses this to pull images and start sandbox containers. Read-write: we create + tear down containers.

Config.Name: Max upload (MiB)
Config.Description: Hard cap on archive size. Default 2 GiB.

Config.Name: Run timeout (minutes)
Config.Description: Auto-stop the sandboxed container after this many minutes.

Config.Name: Admin Username
Config.Description: Optional. Seed the admin username non-interactively. Leave blank to create via the first-run wizard.

Config.Name: Admin Password
Config.Description: Optional. Seed the admin password non-interactively. Leave blank (recommended) to create via the first-run wizard.
